name: Johnsonville Tva Employees
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union serving TVA Johnsonville plant employees and families since 1952, offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans with $168M in assets.
description_long:
Johnsonville TVA Employees Credit Union was established in 1952 and serves a specialized membership base of Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) plant employees in Johnsonville and affiliated select employee groups, plus their family members. The institution is state-chartered and NCUA-insured, providing the same deposit protection as traditional banks. With approximately $168.14 million in assets, $140.22 million in deposits, and $89.91 million in loans outstanding, it operates as a mid-sized regional credit union headquartered in Camden, Tennessee.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 1 to 5 years, mortgages for both home purchases and refinancing (15-year and 30-year options), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, personal loans, and other standard deposit and lending products. They maintain competitive rates: savings at 4.20% APY, 1-year CDs at 4.59% APY, and 30-year mortgages at 4.91% APR according to their listed rates.

What distinguishes Johnsonville TVA Employees Credit Union is its targeted field of membership—it exclusively serves employees of the TVA Johnsonville facility and their families, creating a tight-knit community-focused institution rather than a general-public credit union. This membership restriction allows the credit union to align its products and services with the specific financial needs of TVA workers. Additionally, as a member-owned cooperative, depositors benefit from credit union structure advantages including potential for better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks.

Honestly, this credit union has limitations for most consumers: the restricted membership means only TVA employees and their immediate families can join, making it inaccessible to the general public. The website content shows incomplete rate information and indicates they are still populating deposit and loan rates in their database system, suggesting potential gaps in digital transparency. For eligible members, it represents a solid regional option, but the narrow field of membership severely restricts its applicability. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts with competitive APY rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with 1-year, 2-year, and 5-year terms
Deposit accounts with NCUA insurance protection
Member services for TVA employee groups and families
pros: NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Member-owned credit union structure typically offering better rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Established 74-year history since 1952 providing stability and local presence in Tennessee
Competitive rates on savings (4.20% APY) and 1-year CDs (4.59% APY) as of April 2026
Diversified product offerings including mortgages, HELOCs, auto loans, and personal loans
$168.14 million in assets providing institutional stability for a regional credit union
Specialized focus on TVA employee financial needs creates tailored service approach
cons: Restricted membership—only TVA Johnsonville plant employees, affiliated groups, and their families can join; inaccessible to general public
Incomplete rate information on website; website indicates rates are still being populated in the system as of April 2026
Limited branch network—only one headquarters location listed (Camden, TN) with mention of additional branches but no specific locations provided
Reduced digital transparency compared to larger national credit unions; deposit and loan rate details unavailable online
Small asset base ($168M) compared to major regional or national credit unions, potentially limiting product sophistication
best_for: Current and former TVA Johnsonville plant employees seeking member-owned financial institution benefits
Families of TVA employees who qualify for membership through relative enrollment
Johnsonville area residents employed by TVA partner companies and affiliated select employee groups
Conservative depositors prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and community-focused banking
diagnosis: Johnsonville TVA Employees Credit Union is exclusively suited for current and former TVA Johnsonville plant employees and their eligible family members seeking a stable, member-owned financial institution with competitive rates and NCUA protection. The critical caveat is membership eligibility—if you don't work for TVA or aren't related to someone who does, you cannot join regardless of product quality or rates.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: NCUA-insured credit union serving TVA Johnsonville employees and families since 1952, offering savings, CDs, and mortgages.
